title: Business Application Schema
description: Schema for defining business concepts and their relationships

fields.description: Fields used to generate the presentation ID

separator.description: Separator used between fields in presentation ID

show.description: Whether to show the presentation ID in the UI

documents.description: Document management configuration for this concept

enabled.description: Whether document management is active for this concept

subtype.description: Subtype for fields. 'part_of'/'related_to' for relations, 'money' for decimal, '<concept>.<plural_name>' for prefill.

on_delete.description: Deletion behavior for a relation_to_one. Defaults to 'cascade' when required is true and to 'set_null' when required is false. 'cascade' deletes referencing records; 'set_null' clears the foreign key and requires an optional relation; 'snapshot_data' first stores the referenced row as JSON in an internal _<field>_deleted_snapshot column and then clears the foreign key, so it also requires an optional relation.

required.description:
Whether the field is required. Defaults to false, except for relation_to_one with subtype 'part_of' where it defaults to true.

  'ask_after_login' is only allowed on plain (non-relation, non-calculated) fields of a profile concept (a concept referenced by security roles[].profile_concept). The column stays nullable in the database so the profile can be auto-created at login, but the UI treats the field as mandatory: forms validate it as required, and after login the application asks the user to fill any of these fields that are still empty. Once a value is set, a trigger prevents updating it back to null. The field must be writable by the profile's role.

unique.description: Whether the field must be unique

default.description:
Default value for the field. Supported forms:

  Literal — the value is emitted as the SQL column DEFAULT.

  from_metadata(key[, key...]) — only on string fields of a profile concept (a concept referenced by security roles[].profile_concept). When the profile row is auto-created at login, the field is filled with the first non-empty value among the given keys of the auth token's user_metadata; each key is looked up at the top level and inside the custom_claims object, where Supabase nests non-standard OIDC claims. Keys therefore cover both self-registration (signUp options.data, e.g. 'first_name') and SSO providers (e.g. Keycloak's 'given_name'). No SQL DEFAULT is emitted; when every key is empty the field is left null. E.g. 'from_metadata(first_name, given_name)'. The field must be writable by the profile's role and cannot be required (use 'ask_after_login' to collect missing values after login).

size.description: Size of text field (s, m, l) - only applicable to string fields

precision.description: Type-specific precision: total digits for decimal fields, or 'minute'/'second' display and input precision for datetime fields.

calculated.description:
Marks a field as backend-managed and read-only in the UI. Supported forms:

  SQL expression — any PostgreSQL expression referencing sibling columns, e.g. 'quantity*unit_price'. Stored as a GENERATED ALWAYS AS column; cannot be written by the user.

  rollup(func, child_concept, child_field) — aggregates child_field across all records of child_concept that are part_of this concept. func is one of: sum, count, avg, min, max. The result is kept in sync by triggers on the child table. E.g. 'rollup(sum,order_item,total_price)'.

  copy(fk_field, source_field, when) — copies source_field from the concept referenced by fk_field. fk_field must be a relation_to_one field on the same concept. when controls when the copy fires and can be a single event or several joined with '+': 'on_change' (BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE), 'on_insert' (BEFORE INSERT), 'on_change_in_state_<name>' (when the relevant concept changes while in that workflow state or reaches that workflow state). For state events on a concept without its own workflow the parent part_of concept's state is used: the value is copied both when a new row is inserted while the parent is already in that state, and when an existing parent transitions into that state. Changing fk_field while the parent is in a matching state also triggers a recopy. Direct writes to the field are blocked. E.g. 'copy(product,price,on_change_in_state_initial)' or 'copy(warehouse,cost,on_insert+on_change_in_state_confirmed)'.

  copy_logged_on_insert(fk_field) — on INSERT sets fk_field to the id of the authenticated user's profile record for the concept that fk_field points to (which must be a profile concept). No-op when auth.uid() is NULL. Direct updates are blocked. E.g. 'copy_logged_on_insert(customer)'.

  by_rules — value is written by a generated Supabase Edge Function that evaluates a trusted FEEL business rule. Direct user writes are blocked by trigger protection; service_role writes are allowed.
